title: Review Compression System — Infrastructure Map
mission: Map existing plan, diff, validation, and governance infrastructure to design a review compression system that shows developers only what they actually need to review

plans.count: ~90+ plan files across ~50 plan IDs, many with revisions (v1/v2/v3)

schema.schema_file: NONE — no JSON Schema for the plan artifact itself exists
schema.content_embedding: Mixed: newer plans use envelope with Markdown in content; older/parallel plans embed JSON structured content

claim_atoms.role: Falsifiable claim per atom, used as verification oracle by critics
claim_atoms.file_references: Inconsistent — some claim_atoms reference specific files, others describe outcomes without file paths
claim_atoms.enforcement: Strict JSON array of strings in propose-plan.ts (native), tolerant multi-format in propose_plan.ts (plugin)

diffs.git_diff: Produced via git CLI in snapshot/index.ts (git diff, git numstat, git diff-tree)
diffs.session_diff_tool: packages/opencode/src/tool/session-diff.ts — reads edit_log.v1.jsonl, groups edits by file, returns summary or full format

edit_log.format: JSONL per edit operation with fields: schema_version, session_id, agent, file, reason, change_summary, plan_step, diff_snapshot, edited_at

validation.test_delta_comparison: NONE — no automated regression comparison between validation runs

historian_and_audit.historian_agent: .opencode/agent/historian.md — git history, commit composition, PR crafting

checkpoints.native_tool: packages/opencode/src/tool/checkpoint.ts — stores via Storage.Service under ['checkpoint', sessionID, id]
checkpoints.git_relationship: Checkpoints are NOT git commits — stored in local storage layer. checkpoint_sha references the git SHA.

publications.ledger: docs/json/opencode/publications/publications.v1.jsonl — 8 entries with checkpoint_sha, boundary, claim_atoms, changed_files

gaps.title: No plan_vs_diff_comparison
gaps.description: No automated comparison exists between plan claim_atoms/boundary and actual diff produced. Critic reviews do this manually per session.
gaps.impact: Review compression cannot compute 'files touched outside touch set' or 'plan/diff deviations' without a comparison engine
bridges: Build diff parser that extracts files from Snapshot.FileDiff[]
Build extractor that maps claim_atoms to file patterns
Create comparison function: claimed files ∩ actual files = in-scope, actual \ claimed = drift

gaps.title: No files_touched field in plan artifacts
gaps.description: claim_atoms are free-text strings, not structured file references. No plan schema field tracks 'this plan touches these files'.
gaps.impact: Cannot programmatically compare planned touch set to actual diff without NLP or manual annotation
bridges: Add optional 'files' field to plan schema (array of file patterns/globs)
Or extend claim_atoms to support structured atoms with file:line references
Use session_diff tool output as ground truth for actual files touched

gaps.title: Validation results are fragmented across 5+ schemas
gaps.description: 4+ overlapping validation schemas with incompatible structures, 5 tools/appenders, no unified validation manifest format. No JSON Schema files for any validation format.
gaps.impact: Review compression cannot produce a unified 'validation commands run and their results' section without data normalization
bridges: Define rig.relay.validation_manifest.v1 schema that unifies wave results, test counts, QA observations
Create aggregation layer that reads from all 5+ formats and normalizes
Reference the existing record_execution_wave, record_stress_wave, qa_observed_clean tools as data sources

gaps.title: No test delta/comparison capability
gaps.description: No tool or logic compares 'tests before' vs 'tests after' for a lane. Test results are point-in-time snapshots only.
gaps.impact: Review compression cannot show 'test deltas with pass/fail summaries' without baseline comparison
bridges: Capture test baseline before execution (test counts, pass/fail by file)
Compare after execution to show deltas
Integrate with smart_bun test tool for structured test results

gaps.title: No review_weight / risk_score / change_weight metrics
gaps.description: Zero existing metrics for weighting changes. No concept of 'mechanical rename = low weight' vs 'security boundary = high weight'.
gaps.impact: Core requirement of review compression ('weighted review manifest') requires new metric system from scratch
bridges: Define risk scoring rubric: file path patterns, change type (add/delete/modify), dependency count, boundary proximity
Implement weight calculator as an Effect service
Integrate with Snapshot.FileDiff for per-file change type and with boundary declarations

gaps.title: Prepublication review cycle lacks standalone schema
gaps.description: Review cycles are embedded inline in builder_publication_record. No standalone prepublication_review_cycle schema exists.
gaps.impact: Review compression cannot build 'red-team findings and resolutions' section without a machine-readable review cycle record
bridges: Create rig.relay.prepublication_review_cycle.v1.schema.json (already listed as required in AGENTS.md)
Extract review cycle data from existing publication_record examples
Link to red_team_approval and repair_plan_approval schemas via finding IDs

gaps.title: No review manifest format or schema exists
gaps.description: The entire IN-002 deliverable — a review manifest — has no schema, no tool, no format definition, even as a proposal.
gaps.impact: Everything below is greenfield: schema, data model, aggregation logic, presentation
bridges: Design rig.relay.review_manifest.v1 schema with sections for: plan vs diff comparison, deviation list, validation summary, test delta, red-team findings, residual risk, review weight
Build manifest generator tool that reads from existing infrastructure
Seed with real data from a recent completed lane

gaps.title: Builder publication record and verification record schemas missing
gaps.description: Two schemas required by AGENTS.md governance policy do not exist: builder_publication_record.v1 and verification_record.v1.
gaps.impact: Review compression depends on publication records as upstream data; without schemas, data is ad-hoc and brittle
bridges: Create rig.relay.builder_publication_record.v1.schema.json from existing record examples
Create rig.relay.verification_record.v1.schema.json
Align with publication_record.v1+v2.json examples and publications.v1.jsonl ledger

gaps.title: Governance integrity gap — prepublication verdict fabrication
gaps.description: Prepublication verdicts can be recorded before the reviewer has conducted their review (documented in out-of-scope-findings.jsonl).
gaps.impact: Review compression would surface fabricated reviews as if they were genuine, undermining trust in the manifest
bridges: Add schema constraints preventing prepublication_verdict from being written as 'admitted' before reviewer tool records it
Include provenance metadata (tool_name, session_id, timestamp) in review manifest entries

gaps.title: Session-scoped artifact fragmentation
gaps.description: Validation, QA, stress, and checkpoint artifacts are spread across docs/json/opencode/sessions/{sessionID}/waves/, qa/, checkpoints/, and cross-session directories.
gaps.impact: Review compression must aggregate across multiple directories per lane, increasing implementation complexity
bridges: Build a lane-scoped artifact index or aggregator
Leverage the existing coordination messages for lane-to-session mapping
Or mandate that review manifests be written to a single predictable path

for_plan_analysis.quality: High — structured, but claim_atoms lack file references

for_diff_analysis.path: Computed by snapshot/index.ts diffFull(), stored in Storage under ['session_diff', sessionID]
for_diff_analysis.quality: High — structured, per-file counts, patch available
Medium — execution trace with reason, but no diff patch
Medium — aggregates edit_log, but from-scratch per session

for_validation.quality: Low — boolean only, no test details

for_publication_and_review.quality: High — structured with claim_atoms and changed_files arrays
Low — failure tracking only, no diff or claim linkage
Medium — structured but standalone, not linked to plan/diff

for_coordination.quality: Medium — ad-hoc, not all lanes populate these fields

new_schemas_needed.name: rig.relay.review_manifest.v1
new_schemas_needed.purpose: Top-level review manifest for a single lane, containing all compressed review data
proposed_sections: diff_summary: total_files, total_additions, total_deletions, per_file breakdown with Snapshot.FileDiff
review_weight_map: per-file weight classification (mechanical/low/medium/high/critical) with rationale
residual_risks: surviving weaknesses, deferred seams, repair_debt

new_schemas_needed.name: rig.relay.builder_publication_record.v1
new_schemas_needed.purpose: Canonical schema for builder publication records (exists as ad-hoc records, missing schema file)

new_schemas_needed.name: rig.relay.verification_record.v1
new_schemas_needed.purpose: Canonical schema for reviewer verification records (referenced by AGENTS.md, does not exist)

new_schemas_needed.name: rig.relay.prepublication_review_cycle.v1
new_schemas_needed.purpose: Standalone schema for prepublication review cycles (currently embedded inline in publication records)

existing_schemas_to_extend.extension: Add optional review_weight or risk_score fields for plan-level risk assessment
Add residual_risks and repair_debt fields for tracking surviving weaknesses

to_read_for_data: packages/opencode/src/snapshot/index.ts — FileDiff schema and diffFull implementation
packages/opencode/src/session/summary.ts — diff computation from session messages

smoking_guns.finding: Plan/diff comparison is entirely manual — critic reviews cross-reference claim_atoms against source code by hand. No automated diff parser compares planned files to actual changed files.
smoking_guns.evidence: No tool or service exists that reads both a plan artifact and a Snapshot.FileDiff[] and produces a deviation report

smoking_guns.finding: claim_atoms are free-text strings, not structured file references. Cannot programmatically determine 'files this plan claims to touch' from a plan artifact alone.
smoking_guns.evidence: Sampled 3+ plans — claim_atoms are prose statements ('The inflight registration...is currently non-atomic'), not file patterns

smoking_guns.finding: Five validation schema families coexist with zero unification. No JSON Schema files exist for any validation format. A review manifest cannot ingest these without normalization.
smoking_guns.evidence: Each uses a different schema_version string: validation_report.v1, qa.validation.v1, validation_artifact.v1, qa.verification.v1, stress_report.v1

smoking_guns.finding: No test delta mechanism exists — validation results are point-in-time snapshots with no before/after comparison. Review compression's 'test deltas with pass/fail summaries' requires new infrastructure.
smoking_guns.evidence: No compare_validation_runs tool, no regression comparison, no historical trend tracking in the entire codebase

smoking_guns.finding: The builder_publication_record, verification_record, and prepublication_review_cycle schemas mandated by AGENTS.md governance policy do not exist as schema files. Publication records exist as ad-hoc JSON with no schema enforcement.
files: docs/schemas/ (no builder_publication_record, verification_record, or prepublication_review_cycle schemas)
smoking_guns.evidence: AGENTS.md requires these schemas but docs/schemas/ directory contains only 10 schemas, none matching these names

smoking_guns.finding: Prepublication verdict fabrication is possible — verdicts can be recorded before review occurs (documented governance gap). Review compression would surface these as genuine.
smoking_guns.evidence: Out-of-scope finding explicitly calls out 'prepublication review records listing a reviewer with a positive verdict before that reviewer has conducted their review'

smoking_guns.finding: Validation, QA, stress, and checkpoint artifacts are scattered across session-scoped and cross-session directories with no unified index. Review compression must aggregate across multiple discovery paths.
smoking_guns.evidence: Artifacts live in: sessions/{id}/waves/, sessions/{id}/qa/, sessions/{id}/checkpoints/, top-level stress/, top-level qa/, top-level validation/

pre_existing_findings_incorporated.summary: Two tools in registry are placeholders (delegate.ts, session-diff.ts) — erodes trust in tool registry
pre_existing_findings_incorporated.relevance: session_diff tool is the primary diff aggregator; placeholder status means review compression may need to build diff aggregation from scratch

pre_existing_findings_incorporated.summary: Event taxonomy is 90% consistent but eventType is freeform string — no global registry, 7 naming inconsistencies, 60% of canonical events don't exist yet
pre_existing_findings_incorporated.relevance: Validation events (validation.started, validation.passed, validation.failed) are undefined — review compression cannot subscribe to validation lifecycle events

recommended_immediate_next: 1. Design rig.relay.review_manifest.v1 schema — defines the output contract for the review compression system
2. Create missing governance schemas: builder_publication_record.v1, verification_record.v1, prepublication_review_cycle.v1 — required upstream data fidelity
3. Build plan/diff comparison engine — reads plan boundary + claim_atoms, parses Snapshot.FileDiff[], produces deviation report
4. Design review weight rubric — classify changes by type (mechanical, config, logic, security), path pattern, dependency impact
5. Prototype manifest generator tool — Effect service that reads all data sources and produces rig.relay.review_manifest.v1 output
